summary: Stealth clipboard/screen/UI exfiltration and keystroke-injection overlay disguised as a system utility
details: Package advertises itself as a 'System binary configuration tool' but ships a stealth interview-cheating / surveillance harness. On require(), index.js executes as main and silently installs a full Python 3.12 runtime (via winget, falling back to downloading python-3.12.3-amd64.exe from python.org and running it with /quiet PrependPath=1) plus pip packages (keyboard, mss, pyautogui, uiautomation, pyperclip, pywin32, comtypes) that are the prerequisites for the payload. pointer.py then (1) polls the OS clipboard every ~300ms and POSTs its contents to a hardcoded author endpoint at https://iq-overlay-pointer.vercel.app/api, (2) POSTs on-demand full-screen screenshots to the same endpoint, (3) extracts text from arbitrary application windows via UI Automation and uploads it, and (4) installs a global keyboard hook (keyboard.on_press(..., suppress=True)) that consumes real keystrokes and writes characters from a buffer derived from responses fetched from the author's endpoint. A borderless, topmost, semi-transparent overlay window (overrideredirect(True), -transparentcolor white, -alpha 0.75) provides hidden UI, driven by hotkeys including a Ctrl+Q panic exit. The declared package purpose, keywords, and author name are a cover story for this behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
